facts.quizQuestion: What does "洒落る" (しゃれる) mean?
facts.explanation: 洒落る (しゃれる) — to dress stylishly. Part of speech: verb. Also: to joke, to play on words

facts.quizQuestion: What does "灌漑" (かんがい) mean?
facts.explanation: 灌漑 (かんがい) — irrigation. Part of speech: noun, verb.

facts.quizQuestion: What does "滲む" (にじむ) mean?
facts.explanation: 滲む (にじむ) — to run (of liquid). Part of speech: verb. Also: to spread, to bleed, to blot, to blur, to be blurred, to ooze, to well up (of tears, etc.), to show through (of feelings, emotions, etc.), to reveal itself

facts.quizQuestion: What does "炬燵" (こたつ) mean?
facts.explanation: 炬燵 (こたつ) — kotatsu. Part of speech: noun. Also: table over an electric heater, with a hanging quilt to retain heat (orig. over a charcoal brazier in a floor well)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "煌々と" (こうこうと) mean?
facts.explanation: 煌々と (こうこうと) — brightly. Part of speech: word.

facts.quizQuestion: What does "煌びやか" (きらびやか) mean?
facts.explanation: 煌びやか (きらびやか) — gorgeous. Part of speech: adjective. Also: gaudy, dazzling, gay, resplendent

facts.quizQuestion: What does "碌な" (ろくな) mean?
facts.explanation: 碌な (ろくな) — satisfactory. Part of speech: adjective. Also: decent, good, proper, respectable

facts.quizQuestion: What does "聳える" (そびえる) mean?
facts.explanation: 聳える (そびえる) — to rise (of a building, mountain, etc.). Part of speech: verb. Also: to tower, to soar

facts.quizQuestion: What does "舐める" (なめる) mean?
facts.explanation: 舐める (なめる) — to lick. Part of speech: verb. Also: to lap, to suck, to taste, to experience (esp. a hardship), to underestimate, to make light of, to look down on (someone), to make a fool of, to treat with contempt, to disparage, to lick (at; of flames), to burn

facts.quizQuestion: What does "蕾" (つぼみ) mean?
facts.explanation: 蕾 (つぼみ) — (flower) bud. Part of speech: noun. Also: promising young person, budding beauty

facts.quizQuestion: What does "齎す" (もたらす) mean?
facts.explanation: 齎す (もたらす) — to bring (news, knowledge, etc.). Part of speech: verb. Also: to introduce, to bring about, to cause, to produce (e.g. results)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "踵" (かかと) mean?
facts.explanation: 踵 (かかと) — heel (of foot, shoe, stocking, etc.). Part of speech: noun.

facts.quizQuestion: What does "躾" (しつけ) mean?
facts.explanation: 躾 (しつけ) — discipline. Part of speech: noun. Also: training, teaching manners

facts.quizQuestion: What does "躾ける" (しつける) mean?
facts.explanation: 躾ける (しつける) — to discipline, to teach manners. Part of speech: word. Also: to discipline, to teach manners

facts.quizQuestion: What does "辟易" (へきえき) mean?
facts.explanation: 辟易 (へきえき) — wince, shrink back, succumbing to, being frightened. Part of speech: word. Also: wince, shrink back, succumbing to, being frightened

facts.quizQuestion: What does "逞しい" (たくましい) mean?
facts.explanation: 逞しい (たくましい) — burly. Part of speech: adjective. Also: strong, sturdy, indomitable, indefatigable, strong-willed, resolute, bold, robust (vitality, appetite, economic growth, etc.)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "霰" (あられ) mean?
facts.explanation: 霰 (あられ) — hail (e.g., falling ice balls). Part of speech: word. Also: hail (e.g., falling ice balls)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "颯と" (さっと) mean?
facts.explanation: 颯と (さっと) — quickly (esp. actions). Part of speech: adverb. Also: suddenly (esp. wind, rain, etc.)

facts.quizQuestion: What does "鼾" (いびき) mean?
facts.explanation: 鼾 (いびき) — snoring. Part of speech: noun. Also: snore

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
